refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve an operational availability equal to or greater than the customer-defined target over the agreed evaluation period.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR was mostly atomic, focusing on meeting or exceeding a customer-defined availability target. The refinement clarifies the requirement by specifying the measurement context (operational availability) and the evaluation period, ensuring the FR remains solution-neutral and focused on what the system must achieve.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the operational availability of the NGHE solution over the agreed evaluation period and compare it against the customer-defined target to verify compliance.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall achieve an operational availability equal to or greater than the customer-defined target over the agreed evaluation period.

candidate_dps: Design system components with redundancy to ensure continuous operation despite failures
Implement real-time monitoring and diagnostics to detect and address faults promptly
Establish preventive maintenance schedules based on usage and condition data
Incorporate fail-safe mechanisms that maintain operation under fault conditions
Define operational policies and procedures that support high availability
selected_dp.selected_dp: Design system components with redundancy to ensure continuous operation despite failures

potential_sources: Redundancy design may interact with monitoring systems if not properly modularized
coupling_check.notes: Selected DP focuses on redundancy to achieve availability independently; monitoring and maintenance can be modularized separately to avoid coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes redundancy can be implemented without excessive cost or complexity
Risk that redundancy alone may not meet availability if maintenance and monitoring are insufficient
Operational policies must align with technical design to realize availability targets
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including monitoring and maintenance DPs to complement redundancy and ensure comprehensive availability management.

im_boundary.name: Safety Services
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for ensuring system reliability and continuous operation through redundancy and failure management

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Sends alerts about system failures and redundancy status to other modules

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives system status data and sends redundancy health metrics

module_containment_scores.notes: Very broad module; contains many functions beyond redundancy, leading to high diffusion.
Physical system module; redundancy design logic is only a part, causing significant diffusion.
Focused on remote control; redundancy design is peripheral, poor containment.
Core responsibility includes redundancy and failure management; best containment with minimal diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The redundancy design DP is best contained within the Safety Services module, which specializes in system reliability and failure management, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with Vehicle module if physical redundancy mechanisms are tightly coupled.
Interfaces with Teleoperation and NGHE Solution may require careful coordination to avoid redundancy logic duplication.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP logically addresses the FR by specifying redundancy in system components to achieve the required operational availability target, aligning with the acceptance criteria of availability ≥ 0.97.

diffusion_risks: Potential overlap with Vehicle module if physical redundancy mechanisms are tightly coupled.
Interfaces with Teleoperation and NGHE Solution may require careful coordination to avoid redundancy logic duplication.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The Safety Services module shows the best containment for the redundancy DP with a high containment score (9) and low diffusion score (2), indicating minimal diffusion and strong cohesion. Other modules have higher diffusion scores, indicating risk of scattered redundancy logic.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of the FR through the IM is feasible as Safety Services module provides clear interfaces (SafetyAlert and Telemetry) to monitor redundancy health and system failures, enabling measurement of operational availability against the target.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the redundancy DP to the Safety Services IM is clear and well-justified, with the module's scope explicitly covering reliability and failure management, supporting traceability from FR to IM.

recommended_revisions: Explicitly define coordination protocols between Safety Services and Vehicle and Teleoperation modules to prevent redundancy logic duplication and manage overlap risks.
Document interface contracts and data exchange formats for SafetyAlert and Telemetry to ensure consistent implementation and verification.
Consider refining the NGHE Solution module to reduce its broad scope and high diffusion, possibly by delegating redundancy-related responsibilities fully to Safety Services.

decision.justification: Although all gates have passed, the high safety criticality and identified diffusion risks necessitate revisions to ensure robust coordination and clear interface definitions. Explicitly defining coordination protocols and interface contracts will mitigate risks of redundancy logic duplication and overlap, enhancing system safety and maintainability before proceeding to further decomposition.

revise.why: To explicitly define coordination protocols between Safety Services and other modules, document interface contracts and data exchange formats, and refine module scopes to reduce diffusion and overlap risks.

assumptions: Safety Services module is the primary owner of redundancy logic.
Interfaces SafetyAlert and Telemetry provide sufficient observability for verification.
open_risks: Potential overlap with Vehicle module if physical redundancy mechanisms are tightly coupled.
Interfaces with Teleoperation and NGHE Solution may cause redundancy logic duplication.
evidence_needed: Detailed coordination protocols between Safety Services, Vehicle, and Teleoperation modules.
Documented interface contracts and data exchange formats for SafetyAlert and Telemetry.
Refined scope and responsibilities of NGHE Solution module to confirm reduced diffusion.
